system.description:
The caster shapes quasi-real illusions resembling monsters, that can attack the caster's foes. The shadow conjurations remain semi-solid, even to those disbelieving them. The caster can create one or more illusions of creatures whose total normal HD are equal to the caster's level. The caster chooses what form the illusions take, such as a goblin or dwarf, but the size of the creatures must be small or medium. These shadow creatures have only 20% the normal HP of a creature of the chosen type.

Characters interacting with these illusions believe them to be real creatures unless they make a successful intelligence save. If the saving throw fails, the creatures deal normal damage and have all the normal abilities and weaknesses of a creature of that type. If the saving throw succeeds, the creatures' special abilities, armor class, damage and all other aspects are only 20% as strong as the real thing. Those who succeed at their saves see the conjurations as transparent images superimposed on vague, shadowy forms.
